---
title: "Publications"
---

## Peer-Reviewed Articles

::: {.pub-entry}
[Assessing Border Walls' Varied Impacts on Terrorist Group Diffusion]{.pub-title}\
[Andrew S. Rosenberg and Nazli Avdan]{.pub-authors}\
[Conflict Management and Peace Science, 2024]{.pub-venue}

[Examines how barriers affect the spread of violence among extremist organizations between 1970 and 2017 using network analysis.]{.pub-blurb}

::: {.pub-links}
[DOI](https://doi.org/10.1177/07388942241270927)
:::
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Race and Systemic Crises in International Politics: An Agenda for Pluralistic Scholarship]{.pub-title}\
[Andrew S. Rosenberg]{.pub-authors}\
[Review of International Studies 50(3): 457–475, 2024]{.pub-venue}

[Argues that racial inequality intersects with climate change, inequality, and migration in 21st-century international crises.]{.pub-blurb}

::: {.pub-links}
[DOI](https://doi.org/10.1017/S0260210523000761)
:::
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Where There's a Will, There's a Way: Border Walls and Refugees]{.pub-title}\
[Nazli Avdan, Andrew S. Rosenberg, and Christopher Gelpi]{.pub-authors}\
[Journal of Peace Research, 2023]{.pub-venue}

[Tests whether border fencing effectively reduces refugee flows between 1970 and 2017.]{.pub-blurb}

::: {.pub-links}
[DOI](https://doi.org/10.1177/00223433231200918)
:::
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Racial Discrimination in International Visa Policies]{.pub-title}\
[Andrew S. Rosenberg]{.pub-authors}\
[International Studies Quarterly 67(2): sqad032, 2023]{.pub-venue}

[Analyzes bilateral visa waiver policies from 1973 to 2013 to demonstrate racial bias in mobility rights.]{.pub-blurb}

::: {.pub-links}
[DOI](https://doi.org/10.1093/isq/sqad032)
:::
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Agents, Structures, and the Moral Basis of Deportability]{.pub-title}\
[Andrew S. Rosenberg]{.pub-authors}\
[Security Dialogue 54(6): 602–619, 2022]{.pub-venue}

[Examines how state agency and citizen beliefs produce policies that entrench migrant precarity.]{.pub-blurb}

::: {.pub-links}
[DOI](https://doi.org/10.1177/09670106221113183)
:::
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Democratization and Representative Bureaucracy — An Analysis of Promotion Patterns in Indonesia's Civil Service, 1980–2015]{.pub-title}\
[Jan H. Pierskalla, Adam Lauretig, Andrew S. Rosenberg, and Audrey Sacks]{.pub-authors}\
[American Journal of Political Science 65(2): 261–277, 2020]{.pub-venue}

[Shows that democratization worsened career prospects for female and minority bureaucrats in Indonesia.]{.pub-blurb}

::: {.pub-links}
[DOI](https://doi.org/10.1111/ajps.12536)
:::
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Measuring Racial Bias in International Migration Flows]{.pub-title}\
[Andrew S. Rosenberg]{.pub-authors}\
[International Studies Quarterly 63(4): 837–845, 2019]{.pub-venue}

[Develops a measurement tool demonstrating that migrants from majority-Black states migrate less than expected.]{.pub-blurb}

::: {.pub-links}
[DOI](https://doi.org/10.1093/isq/sqz039)
:::
:::

::: {.pub-entry}
[Unifying the Study of Asymmetric Hypotheses]{.pub-title}\
[Andrew S. Rosenberg, Austin J. Knuppe, and Bear F. Braumoeller]{.pub-authors}\
[Political Analysis 25(3): 381–401, 2017]{.pub-venue}

[Clarifies asymmetric hypothesis concepts and presents methodologies for testing them empirically.]{.pub-blurb}

::: {.pub-links}
[DOI](https://doi.org/10.1017/pan.2017.16)
[Software](software.qmd#asymmetric)
:::
:::
